forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 55. West southwest wind around 8 mph.

forecast.name: Wednesday
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly sunny, with a high near 62. West southwest wind 7 to 14 mph, with gusts as high as 20 mph.

weather_report:
In Downtown San Francisco, the night air carries a gentle coolness, hinting at the transition from dashed daylight to its intimate counterpart. A layer of clouds blankets the city tonight, creating a muted, ethereal glow, as if the very essence of the evening is wrapped in soft cotton. The temperature hovers around a mild 55 degrees, with a whisper of wind coming from the west-southwest at about eight miles per hour. Beneath this soft canopy, the usual busyness of street life seems subdued, inviting a reflective pause.

As dawn approaches, the forecast promises a transformation. Tomorrow, the clouds will give way to bright sunlit skies, the city revealing itself in shades of vibrant blue and golden hues. Clarity will reign, yet the ever-present wind will gust from 7 to 14 miles per hour, gently tousling hair and rustling jackets. While the heights may be cast in sunshine, the marine layer will linger, cloaking a few coastal edges and probably spilling into the bay, lending a ghostly charm to views of the iconic Golden Gate. Attire will be best suited for layering; a light jacket or sweater shall suffice for the early hours, giving way to short sleeves as the warmth emerges. With the day unfolding promisingly, a leisurely stroll through the Mission District may beckon, where the vibrancy of street art and the allure of boutique coffee shops await, perfectly complementing the spirit of this fine June day.
